title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Automation and Data Client Systems · Program Defense
summary: A production data release board evaluating a changed source contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for response status, success variants, failure taxonomy, and problem details, producing typed response and failure classifier. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers typed response and failure classifier. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Handle 201, 202, 204, 206, and other success responses with their location, body, and completion implications. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using represent and partial plus specific case evidence.
